categories.title: Source Intake & Content Inventory
categories.summary: The repo needs a clear inventory of what comes from `doc-web` and what comes from separate family-archive sources.

categories.title: Canonical Content Model & Transformation
categories.summary: The repo should turn imported source material into canonical site data rather than binding directly to the first presentation format it receives.

categories.title: Information Architecture & Navigation
categories.summary: The site should make the book easy to traverse by chapter, topic, and companion material without losing narrative continuity.

categories.title: Companion Media & Archive Materials
categories.summary: The website should package scans, audio, and reunion extras as first-class materials without obscuring what belongs to the core book.

categories.title: Site Experience & Presentation
categories.summary: This repo eventually needs a real website runtime and design system, but it does not have one yet.

categories.title: Provenance, Editorial Controls & Publishing
categories.summary: The repo should make imports, changes, and publication decisions inspectable.

categories.title: Accessibility, Performance & Delivery
categories.summary: The final site should be readable, fast enough, and robust across devices and connection quality.

categories.title: AI Harnesses & Content Ops
categories.summary: AI should reduce repetitive cleanup and comparison work, but the outputs still need inspectable records and human review where the archive demands it.

categories.title: Planning Infrastructure
categories.summary: The repo needs enough planning structure to survive long gaps and handoffs without turning into process theater.

coverage_matrix.notes: Canonical machine-readable inventory of upstream content surfaces and companion archive inputs.

sequencing_bias.reason: Define the import seam from doc-web, prove a canonical content model, then build the first real website slice instead of picking a frontend stack in the abstract.

campaigns.notes: Early work should establish the upstream import contract, the site data model, and one end-to-end chapter rendering path with linked media.

sections.title: Recommended Order
sections.markdown: Current priority: lock the `doc-web` import contract, define the canonical site content model, and prove one chapter-plus-media slice before spreading effort across extra archive surfaces or design polish.

upstream_integration.label: Upstream Integration
upstream_integration.notes: Primary risk surface while the repo decides how it consumes doc-web output.

content_model.label: Content Model
content_model.notes: Chapter, section, media, and provenance structures should stabilize before wide UI work.

site_experience.label: Site Experience
site_experience.notes: No frontend substrate exists yet; revisit after the first real rendering slice.

methodology_tooling.label: Methodology Tooling
methodology_tooling.notes: Keep the imported methodology package honest and lightweight for this repo.

surfaces.label: Onward book core HTML
surfaces.notes: Primary authored book body for the website.

surfaces.label: Chapter audiobook links
surfaces.notes: Expected to attach to specific chapter pages.

surfaces.label: Chapter podcast episodes
surfaces.notes: Podcast material connected to individual chapters.

surfaces.label: Full book podcast
surfaces.notes: Long-form podcast coverage for the whole book.

surfaces.label: Scanned companion materials
surfaces.notes: Scans that shipped with or relate directly to the book.
